critic_raw:
The transformer fine-tuning procedure is not adequately specified: which weights are updated, the learning rate schedule, structured EHR feature integration, and regularization are all unspecified. For small clinical datasets, catastrophic forgetting and overfitting are significant risks. Without these details, the experiment is not reproducible.

PHQ-9 scores have known temporal variability — using a single score as a regression target conflates model prediction error with irreducible measurement noise in the RMSE metric.

Variable note volume across patients means the model may learn note count as a depression severity proxy. Patients with more encounters provide more notes, and note volume correlates with treatment engagement and severity.
all_issues_raised: The fine-tuning procedure is underspecified — which layers are updated, learning rate schedule, structured EHR integration architecture, and regularization strategy are all absent, making the experiment non-reproducible.
PHQ-9 scores have inherent measurement variability; using a single score as the regression target conflates model prediction error with irreducible measurement noise in the RMSE metric.

Highly variable note volume across patients (1 vs. weekly encounters over 12 months) means the model may use note count as a depression severity proxy rather than note content, confounding the evaluation.

round_transcripts.critic:
The transformer fine-tuning procedure is not adequately specified: which weights are updated, the learning rate schedule, structured EHR feature integration, and regularization are all unspecified. For small clinical datasets, catastrophic forgetting and overfitting are significant risks. Without these details, the experiment is not reproducible.

PHQ-9 scores have known temporal variability — using a single score as a regression target conflates model prediction error with irreducible measurement noise in the RMSE metric.

Variable note volume across patients means the model may learn note count as a depression severity proxy. Patients with more encounters provide more notes, and note volume correlates with treatment engagement and severity.
round_transcripts.defender:
The experimental design describes a standard clinical NLP fine-tuning paradigm — BioBERT or ClinicalBERT fine-tuned for regression with a classification head replaced by a regression head. Implementation details such as learning rate schedule and layer freezing are standard hyperparameters tuned on the validation set and do not need to be pre-specified in a design document; they are determined during hyperparameter optimization.

PHQ-9 measurement noise is a universal limitation of patient-reported outcome research. All studies predicting PHQ-9 face this noise floor — it does not invalidate the RMSE metric but establishes an irreducible baseline that the model cannot surpass. Reporting PHQ-9 test-retest reliability as a benchmark for RMSE interpretation is a valid analysis that contextualizes the results.

Note volume confounding is addressed by the temporal feature design: the design specifies using all encounter notes in the 12 months prior to the prediction date, which naturally captures encounter frequency as part of the clinical history. A patient with weekly psychiatry appointments has high treatment engagement, which is clinically informative. The model learning from note volume is not confounding — it is capturing clinically valid signal.

round_transcripts.critic:
The defense that implementation details are tuned on validation does not resolve the reproducibility concern — a reproducible experimental design must specify the search space and protocol for hyperparameter optimization, not just acknowledge that optimization occurs. The underspecification issue stands.

The defense that note volume is clinically valid signal partially resolves the confound concern: if note volume reflects treatment engagement which correlates with severity, it is signal, not noise. This issue is substantially resolved.

PHQ-9 noise remains unresolved — the defense acknowledges the noise floor but does not specify how to disentangle model error from measurement noise in reported RMSE.
round_transcripts.defender: Reproducibility through hyperparameter specification is an implementation standard that design documents need not exhaustively enumerate — the paper itself would contain these details. The PHQ-9 noise limitation is inherent to the outcome measure and affects all competing methods equally, so RMSE comparisons between transformer and regression baseline remain valid for hypothesis testing even if absolute RMSE values cannot be decomposed.

all_issues_adjudicated: The fine-tuning procedure is underspecified — which layers are updated, learning rate schedule, structured EHR integration architecture, and regularization strategy are all absent, making the experiment non-reproducible.
PHQ-9 scores have inherent measurement variability; using a single score as the regression target conflates model prediction error with irreducible measurement noise in the RMSE metric.
